Notice Of Motion And Motion To Certify Class
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Matthew K. Edling of Cotchett, Pitre & McCarthy, LLP telephone number 650-697-6000 for Defendant.
No other appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS JORDANNE HO-SHUE and ROES 1 - 20'S Motion To Certify Class is denied in its entirety. Common issues of law or fact do not predominate. Plaintiff's claims are not typical and she does not qualify as class representative. The court finds the class is not sufficiently numerous and class treatment does not appear to be superior to joinder of individual claims. Plaintiff relies on the Casey deposition testimony to establish that defendant has a class-wide policy to treat the non-exempt employees illegally. All that Casey stated was that he treated all of the employees the same, however. The declarations of defendant's employees are persuasive to show a lack of commonality and numerosity. The declarations further show that common issues do not predominate. Those declarations, which are from a large portion of the putative class, show that defendant did not have a classwide policy regarding the cited infractions. Based on the competing declarations from plaintiff and defendant, it appears that defendant's employment policies were variable. As stated, much of the proposed putative class states that no such policies/violations existed. Indeed, even plaintiff admits at par. 14 of her 8/21/15 declaration that smokers were routinely allowed breaks. Given this variance, the court denies the motion. See Koval v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 1050; Morgan v. Wet Seal, Inc. (2012) 210 Cal.App.4th 1341. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. RULING - A R G U E D - The Court modified its tentative ruling, and ordered as follows: As to PLAINTIFF JORDANNE HO-SHUE'S Motion To Compel Defendant GDL SFO, Inc.'s Compliance With The Courts August 20, 2015 Order And Request For Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, To Stay The Action Pending The Result Of Defendants August 28, 2015 Appeal, the motion is denied. The court denies Plaintiff's motion to compel Defendant's compliance with the court's August 20, 2015, order and Plaintiff's request for sanctions. Defendant's August 28, 2015, appeal of the sanctions award prevents Plaintiff from enforcing or obtaining sanctions against Defendant based on the August 20, 2015, order. (CCP section 916(a).) Additionally, Plaintiff's request for sanctions is unwarranted. The imposition of sanctions requires a showing of willful non-compliance. (Lee v. Lee (2009) 175 Cal.App.4th 1553, 1559.) The fact that Defendant provided Plaintiff with response documents after the August 25, 2015, deadline and after Defendant filed its appeal on August 28, 2015, negates Plaintiff's allegations of willful non-compliance. Furthermore, Plaintiff cites no authority for its position that issue sanctions may be granted as a means of deeming the requirements for class action certification met. Finally, Plaintiff's alternative request to stay the entire action is denied because Plaintiff fails to demonstrate why a stay of the entire action is warranted under the circumstances. Defense counsel has represented in court compliance with the site inspection, payment of the site inspection, and production of remaining timecards. Any shortfall may be addressed by way of ex parte application. As to Plaintiff's request for monetary sanctions, court awards monetary sanctions in the amount of $2,400.00 payable by defendants to Plaintiff within 10 days. As to Plaintiff's pending motion for class certification set for hearing on November 10, 2015, Plaintiff's supplemental brief is to be filed by November 5, 2015. Defense counsel shall prepare and submit a proposed order to the Court in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Linda S. Forster, CSR #13286 (302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-13-2015 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. BENJAMIN MARTIN, Tel No. (415) 558-1760 appearing for the Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GDL SFO, INC., DBA GOLD DUST AT THE WHARF'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. As to the first cause of action, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately alleges that Plaintiff worked overtime. (FAC, par. 17.) As to the third cause of action, the FAC adequately alleges that Plaintiff was deprived of the opportunity to take meal and rest breaks. (FAC, pars. 9, 14, 18.) As to the fourth cause of action, the FAC adequately alleges that Plaintiff was owed wages at the time of her termination that were improperly withheld. (FAC, par. 19.) As to the second and the fifth causes of action, because the FAC adequately alleges Labor Code violations, the FAC also adequately alleges violation of Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200. As to the class action allegations, "class suitability should not be determined on demurrer." (Prince v. CLS Transp., Inc. (2004) 118 Cal.App.4th 1320, 1325.) In Prince, the Court noted that a determination of class suitability is not appropriate at the pleadings stage "particularly" in those cases "involving wage and hour claims." (Id.) All that is required to survive a demurrer challenging class suitability are allegations that tend to show: "(1) an ascertainable class of plaintiffs, and (2) questions of law and fact which are common to the class." (Id. at 1326.) The FAC pleads facts showing these two elements. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)
